name: DOGE 4h Retest Hold Timeout v1
edge_mechanism: a structural break is retested and held, but exposure is cut by time if expansion does not appear quickly, avoiding slow bleed continuation trades
thesis: Cycle 79 explicitly asks for retest-and-hold continuation that de-risks by time if expansion fails to appear fast enough. DOGE 4h is the best-fit lane because transition structure should produce retests with enough activity to test timeout discipline without forcing 1h noise. The edge is the held retest plus strict time discipline, not passive hope.
invalidation_condition: If DOGE 4h retest holds are too sparse or if time-based de-risking still cannot prevent bleed, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks a local boundary, retests that level, holds it, and should expand quickly if the continuation is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Real continuation often shows itself quickly after an accepted retest. If expansion does not appear fast, the trade is often wrong or stale. Timeout de-risking should reduce that failure mode.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is the best fit because it is the primary lane, transition-heavy enough to generate retests, and less structurally anti-trend than ETH 4h.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: DOGE may still be too rotational, turning retest-hold setups into false positives even with timeout discipline.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but timeout is too aggressive, extend the de-risk clock slightly before loosening the retest filter.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Plenty of retest attempts, many early scratches, and a minority of fast-expanding continuations that justify the structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: DOGE 4h clears density and timeout de-risking meaningfully reduces slow-bleed continuation losses.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or a profile where retest entries still bleed despite the timeout rule.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because retest-hold logic is simpler than delayed-retest continuation theater

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit transition lane for timeout-protected retest continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE 4h shows enough retest-hold events and timeout de-risking reduces bleed.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h remains too sparse or too rotational for the structure to matter.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with quick feedback on whether continuation is real.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same timeout-protected retest grammar survives on the flatter similar lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves enough retest-hold continuation with cleaner structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h becomes too flat or too sparse for the mechanism.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner, slightly lower-count retest continuations than DOGE.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same timeout management works on a stronger trend lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows coherent retest-hold continuation with fewer slow bleeds.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the idea is too dependent on DOGE-style transition churn.

long: Time-based de-risk: cut 50% after 4 bars if no 0.9 ATR expansion
short: Time-based de-risk: cut 50% after 4 bars if no 0.9 ATR expansion
